# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Python Application CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'
          cache: 'pip'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

      - name: Lint with flake8
        run: |
          pip install flake8
          flake8 . --count --select=E9,F63,F7,F82 --show-source --statistics
          flake8 . --count --max-complexity=10 --max-line-length=127 --statistics

      - name: Run security checks with bandit
        run: |
          pip install bandit
          bandit -r src/ -f json -o bandit-report.json || true
          bandit -r src/ -ll

      - name: Check dependencies for known vulnerabilities
        run: |
          pip install pip-audit
          pip-audit --strict

      - name: Run tests with pytest
        env:
          PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: '1'
        run: |
          pip install pytest pytest-cov
          pytest tests/ --cov=src --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term-missing -v

      - name: Upload coverage report
        if: success()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-report
          path: coverage.xml
          retention-days: 7

  deploy:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    environment:
      name: production
      url: ${{ vars.DEPLOYMENT_URL }}
    concurrency:
      group: production-deploy
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'
          cache: 'pip'

      - name: Install production dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Build package
        run: |
          pip install build
          python -m build

      - name: Verify package integrity
        run: |
          pip install twine
          twine check dist/*

      - name: Publish to PyPI
        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
        with:
          packages-dir: dist/

      - name: Deploy to production
        env:
          DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}
          DEPLOY_HOST: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$DEPLOY_TOKEN" ] || [ -z "$DEPLOY_HOST" ]; then
            echo "::error::Required deployment secrets are not configured"
            exit 1
          fi

          SANITIZED_HOST=$(echo "$DEPLOY_HOST" | grep -oP '^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9.\-]+$' || true)
          if [ -z "$SANITIZED_HOST" ]; then
            echo "::error::Invalid deploy host format"
            exit 1
          fi

          curl --fail --silent --show-error \
            --max-time 120 \
            --retry 3 \
            --retry-delay 5 \
            -X POST \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${DEPLOY_TOKEN}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"ref\":\"${GITHUB_SHA}\",\"environment\":\"production\"}" \
            "https://${SANITIZED_HOST}/api/deploy"

          echo "Deployment triggered successfully for commit ${GITHUB_SHA}"
